title: geum radiatum
intro: Geum radiatum, also known as the New Zealand geum, is a flowering plant species native to the mountainous regions of New Zealand. It belongs to the rose family and is characterized by its yellow to orange flowers and feathery foliage. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to help soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Geum radiatum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties and provide health benefits such as reducing pain, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
